paper_title: Pollen Production and Flowering Phenology in Male Shrubs

methods.method_name: Pollen Production and Flowering Phenology Recording
methods.description: This method involves recording pollen production levels and flowering phenology of male shrubs over a period of three years to assess reproductive traits and select the best-performing genotypes.

steps.description: Pollen production levels were recorded in 2016 and 2017, and the flowering phenology was recorded in three consecutive years (2016-2018).
steps.searchable_summary: record pollen production and flowering phenology data

steps.description: Peak flowering for the male genotypes was designated as 'early', 'middle', or 'late' relative to the peak flowering of the female plants.
Inflorescence density and production of viable pollen were recorded for new branches.
steps.searchable_summary: record inflorescence density and viable pollen production

steps.description: Six branches were sampled for each shrub, and the length and number of inflorescences for each of these branches were recorded.
steps.searchable_summary: sample branches and record inflorescence data

steps.description: The number of flowers per inflorescence in 10 fully developed inflorescences was recorded.
steps.searchable_summary: record number of flowers per inflorescence

steps.description: To determine the number of pollen grains per flower, 6 to 12 flowers per studied male genotype were harvested before stamen dehiscence.
steps.searchable_summary: harvest flowers and determine pollen grain count

steps.description: The pollen suspension was injected into a hemocytometer, and the total number of pollen grains per flower was determined three times per flower for three flowers.
steps.searchable_summary: use hemocytometer to count pollen grains

steps.description: Pollen stainability/viability was assayed using 2% acetocarmine.

The number of viable pollen grains per 1 m branch was calculated as the number of inflorescences per 1 m branch × the number of flowers per inflorescence × the number of viable pollen grains per flower.

methods.description: Statistical procedures used to analyze the collected data to compare reproductive traits and evaluate relationships between traits.

steps.description: Statistical analyses were performed with JMP 14.0.0 software: one-way analysis of variance (ANOVA) and post-hoc Tukey-Kramer HSD test were applied to compare reproductive traits among the 11 selected lines in 2016 and 2017.
steps.searchable_summary: perform ANOVA and Tukey-Kramer HSD test

steps.description: Pearson correlation analyses were used to evaluate separately the relationships between the same reproductive traits in 2016 and 2017 among the 11 selected lines and between different reproductive traits in 2016 and 2017.
